jobDescriptionTemplates: Maintained uninterrupted 12-hour work sessions with zero bathroom breaks. Colleagues assumed I had ascended.
Pioneered the 'no notifications, no mercy' productivity framework adopted company-wide.
Delivered entire product roadmaps solo over three consecutive weekends. Did not eat.
Established a personal record of 47 tasks completed in a single sitting. The chair still has an imprint.
Consistently submitted deliverables before the brief was even finished being read aloud.
Refused all social invitations from Q1 through Q3 to hit annual targets. Thriving.
Automated 80% of team workflows and spent the remaining time optimizing the automation.

descriptionTemplates: Built a personal productivity OS from scratch in a single weekend. It tracks everything except fun, which has been deprecated.
Wrote, edited, and self-published a 300-page focus methodology book in 72 hours.
Developed a custom browser extension that blocks all non-work tabs and plays lo-fi beats indefinitely.
Graduated a year early by attending every optional lecture, office hour, and study hall. Voluntarily.
Thesis submitted three months ahead of schedule. Committee was unsettled.
Maintained a 4.0 GPA while holding three part-time jobs and sleeping exactly six hours per night.

funFactTemplates: Has not used social media since discovering it takes 23 minutes to regain focus after a notification.
Keeps a laminated copy of their daily schedule in their wallet at all times.
Once finished a 40-hour project in 14 hours and spent the rest of the time starting the next one.
Their Spotify Wrapped is 97% lo-fi hip hop. The other 3% was an accident.

jobDescriptionTemplates: Managed relocation of 14 cats across three rooms. Eight complied. Four staged a counter-operation. Two were never found.
Successfully guided a colony of 9 feral cats to a feeding station using only patience, tuna, and a broken umbrella.
Developed a morning feeding schedule for 11 cats with 11 different dietary opinions. Zero fatalities.
Coordinated a vet visit for 6 cats simultaneously. Arrived with 6 carriers. Left with 4 cats in carriers and 2 cats in a tote bag.
Redirected a cat who had been sitting on the router for 4 months. Internet speeds improved immediately.
Persuaded a cat to leave a visitor's lap using only indirect eye contact and a crinkle toy. Took 45 minutes.
Established a functional indoor/outdoor transition protocol. Cats remain non-compliant but losses are down 30%.

descriptionTemplates: Designed a room-by-room cat routing system using treat placement and strategic door positioning. Works 40% of the time.
Documented 6 months of cat herding attempts in a behavioral log. Findings were inconclusive. Cats remain in charge.
Built a spreadsheet tracking which cats respond to which sounds, movements, and bribes. Shared widely in local rescue community.

degreeNames: Certificate in Advanced Herding Techniques (Cats Only)
descriptionTemplates: Thesis explored optimal treat-to-compliance ratios across 12 cat breeds. Results were statistically significant but practically useless.
Completed a 200-hour field practicum herding cats at a local shelter. Supervisor noted 'genuine persistence in the face of total indifference.'
Founded the campus Feline Logistics Club. Meetings were frequently disrupted by the one cat allowed in the building.

funFactTemplates: Can identify a cat's exact mood from the position of its tail, ears, and the specific way it is ignoring them.
Has been outsmarted by the same cat seven times. Considers it a learning experience.
Once spent 45 minutes coaxing a cat out from under a bed. The cat left on its own 3 minutes after they gave up.
Owns a herding crook. It has never once worked. They bring it anyway.

jobDescriptionTemplates: Produced best work exclusively between 2am and 5am. Refused to explain why.
Solved a critical production bug while eating cereal directly from the box. Did not use a bowl.
Delivered consistently high-quality output despite a workspace described by HR as 'a fire hazard and a cry for help.'
Invented three internal tools during what was supposed to be a lunch break. Did not take the lunch break.
Attended all video calls from a blanket fort. Results were unimpeachable.
Submitted a 47-page technical report written entirely in one sitting at 3am. It was the best report anyone had ever read.
Optimized a legacy codebase while wearing the same hoodie for five consecutive days. It was a very good hoodie.

descriptionTemplates: Built a fully functional web app during a 72-hour self-imposed isolation sprint. Snacks were pre-staged.
Wrote and shipped an indie game over a long weekend in a room with the curtains permanently closed.
Created an entire personal knowledge base using tools no one has heard of, configured in ways no one would recommend.

schoolNames: Night Owl College of Applied Skills
descriptionTemplates: Attended irregularly but aced every exam by reading the entire textbook in one sleepless pre-exam session.
Completed an online degree entirely at night while holding a day job. The degree is framed; the sleep debt is not.
Audited 34 courses across 6 disciplines. Received credit for none. Retained everything.

funFactTemplates: Their desk has not been fully visible since 2019.
Considers a clean workspace a red flag.
Has a highly specific snack rotation that correlates directly with productivity output.
Once solved a logic puzzle that had stumped the team for a week — while half-asleep and eating cold leftovers.

jobDescriptionTemplates: Overhauled company's visual presence and increased LinkedIn engagement by 340% through strategic headshot selection.
Advised executives on wardrobe alignment with brand values. One refused. He is no longer an executive.
Conducted a full personal brand audit for 12 team members. Results were transformative. One person cried (happy tears).
Established office dress code guidelines that raised team confidence metrics in quarterly surveys.
Prepared client-facing team for a major pitch by running a 2-hour appearance workshop. They won the contract.
Introduced lighting standards for remote video calls that improved perceived professionalism across the board.
Consulted on product photography direction resulting in a 60% increase in click-through rate.

descriptionTemplates: Documented a year-long personal optimization journey across skincare, fitness, and wardrobe into a widely-shared guide.
Developed a 90-day appearance improvement curriculum with measurable milestones and a full photo documentation protocol.
Built a wardrobe system based on capsule principles, seasonal rotation, and aggressive decluttering. Zero regrets.
Won best-dressed award every semester. Accepted it graciously each time while clearly having expected it.
Thesis focused on the measurable impact of appearance on perceived competence. It was very well-presented.
Founded the campus Image Consulting Club. Membership tripled within a semester.

funFactTemplates: Has a 47-step morning routine and finishes it in 38 minutes.
Considers bad lighting a form of sabotage.
Once turned down a job offer because the office had fluorescent lighting.
Their LinkedIn profile photo has been called 'unsettlingly good' by multiple recruiters.

jobDescriptionTemplates: Maintained a live audience of 1,200+ concurrent viewers while simultaneously troubleshooting audio issues and answering chat.
Produced 8-hour marathon streams with consistent viewer retention and only two technical meltdowns per quarter.
Built a loyal community of 40,000 followers through authentic on-camera presence and aggressive scheduling consistency.
Managed live sponsorship integrations without losing chat engagement. A delicate and highly underrated skill.
Developed a live Q&A format that routinely outperformed pre-recorded content in watch time and interaction rate.
Coordinated multi-guest live panels across three timezones with zero pre-roll and minimal chaos.
Grew channel from 200 to 18,000 subscribers in one year through consistent streaming and highly specific niche content.

descriptionTemplates: Built a fully professional streaming studio in a 9x9 room using $600 and an alarming number of zip ties.
Wrote and published a comprehensive guide to starting a stream from zero, covering tech, branding, and parasocial dynamics.
Created a content calendar system used by over 300 independent streamers to manage scheduling and burn-out prevention.
Ran the college's live stream channel for two years, growing viewership by 800% and converting it to a credited program.
Capstone project was a 24-hour charity livestream that raised $12,000 and tested every skill simultaneously.
Studied parasocial relationships academically after observing them professionally. Both experiences were illuminating.

funFactTemplates: Has never watched a show without live-commenting it in some form.
Instinctively says 'welcome back' when someone re-enters a room.
Can read a chat moving at 200 messages per minute and still hold a conversation.
Once streamed through a power outage using a phone hotspot and a laptop on 4% battery. The stream did not end.

jobDescriptionTemplates: Moderated a 200,000-member online community with a team of 6 volunteers and an iron will.
Authored the community guidelines document that reduced rule violations by 65% in the first month of enforcement.
Handled escalations with a calm that made users simultaneously grateful and slightly afraid.
Banned 4,000 accounts in a single weekend during a coordinated raid. Did not flinch.
Developed an escalation matrix for edge-case violations that reduced moderator decision fatigue by 40%.
Maintained neutrality during three separate community-wide controversies. Earned the grudging respect of all parties.
Trained and onboarded 25 new moderators across two platforms, including a written test and a simulated chaos scenario.

descriptionTemplates: Wrote a 60-page community moderation handbook that has been adopted by seven independent online communities.
Built a bot suite to assist with auto-moderation that reduced human review queue by 80% while maintaining accuracy.
Founded a Discord server dedicated to constructive debate with strict rules and surprisingly warm vibes.

degreeNames: M.S. in Online Trust and Safety

descriptionTemplates: Thesis examined the long-term psychological effects of moderation work. Submitted it with the detachment of someone who had seen things.
Founded the university's first Digital Ethics Review Board. Immediately had to moderate a dispute within the board.
Completed a practicum moderating a live forum during finals week. Considered the most stressful real-world simulation available.

funFactTemplates: Reads every Terms of Service document they encounter. Voluntarily.
